name: Building an MCP Server
description: A hands-on course on designing, implementing, and shipping a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that an AI agent like Claude Code can plug into.

pages.name: Welcome
pages.description: Course overview, what you'll build, and prerequisites.

pages.name: What is the Model Context Protocol?
pages.description: MCP in one paragraph, what it solves, and how a server fits in.

pages.name: The Server Lifecycle
pages.description: Initialize, list, call — the request shape an MCP client expects.

pages.name: Project Setup
pages.description: Create the project, install the SDK, and run a no-op server.

pages.name: Defining Tools
pages.description: Tool schemas, input validation, and side-effect safety.

pages.name: Defining Resources
pages.description: Expose read-only data the agent can pull when it needs context.

pages.name: Prompts and Slash Commands
pages.description: Reusable prompt templates the user can invoke from the client.

pages.name: Authentication and Security
pages.description: Auth patterns, secret handling, and the principle of least privilege.

pages.name: Testing the Server
pages.description: Use the MCP Inspector and unit tests to keep behavior honest.
